items.title: AI capex supercycle broadens beyond hyperscalers
items.description: Enterprise and sovereign buyers are now a larger share of AI infrastructure spending than the largest cloud platforms, according to analysts tracking capital-expenditure guidance across the sector. The shift suggests demand for compute capacity is broadening beyond a handful of hyperscalers, with implications for chipmakers, power utilities, and data-center REITs.

items.title: Fed signals gradual path as Warsh settles into chair
items.description: Policymakers signaled a measured, data-dependent approach to further rate moves as the new chair completes his first full quarter at the helm. Officials emphasized continuity with prior guidance while leaving room to adjust if inflation or labor-market data surprise in either direction.

items.title: Chip export rules eased for select allied markets
items.description: Regulators loosened licensing requirements for advanced semiconductor exports to a shortlist of allied economies, a move industry groups say will help chipmakers plan capacity with more certainty. Critics warn the carve-outs could complicate enforcement against transshipment to restricted destinations.

items.title: Mega-cap tech announces multi-billion buyback plan
items.description: A leading technology company unveiled an expanded share-repurchase authorization alongside quarterly results that topped estimates on both revenue and margin. Management pointed to durable free-cash-flow generation and reiterated capital-return priorities alongside continued AI infrastructure investment.

items.title: European regulators open new probe into platform fees
items.description: Brussels opened a fresh investigation into fee structures charged by a major digital platform to third-party sellers, citing concerns the terms may disadvantage smaller merchants. The company said it would cooperate fully and defended its pricing as consistent with market norms.

items.title: EV price war intensifies as new models launch in EU
items.description: A wave of new electric-vehicle launches across Europe has pushed several manufacturers to cut prices further, squeezing margins across the industry. Analysts say scale and battery-cost advantages will determine which entrants can sustain the current pace of discounting.

items.title: Crypto ETF inflows hit multi-month high
items.description: Spot crypto exchange-traded funds recorded their strongest week of net inflows in several months, driven by renewed institutional allocation and lower realized volatility. Issuers said demand was broad-based across both retail brokerage platforms and registered investment advisors.

items.title: Cloud providers report accelerating enterprise demand
items.description: Major cloud infrastructure providers reported bookings growth that outpaced prior quarters, citing renewed enterprise migration activity and expanding AI workloads. Executives flagged ongoing capacity constraints in certain regions as the primary near-term bottleneck to faster revenue recognition.

items.title: Defense budgets revised upward across NATO members
items.description: Several NATO member states raised defense-spending targets for the coming fiscal year, citing evolving security assessments. Analysts expect the revisions to benefit prime contractors and their supply chains, particularly in munitions, air-defense systems, and secure communications.

items.title: Semiconductor capacity expansion announced in Asia
items.description: A major foundry operator confirmed plans to expand advanced-node manufacturing capacity across multiple Asian sites, citing sustained demand from AI accelerator and mobile customers. The expansion is expected to be phased in over the next several years.

items.title: Payments networks report resilient cross-border volume
items.description: Global payments networks reported steady growth in cross-border transaction volume, supported by travel recovery and e-commerce penetration in emerging markets. Executives noted currency volatility as a modest headwind to reported revenue growth.

items.title: Data-center power demand pushes utilities to expand grid capacity
items.description: Regional utilities are accelerating grid-capacity investments to keep pace with data-center power demand tied to AI workloads. Regulators in several jurisdictions are weighing new interconnection rules to balance reliability against the pace of new large-load requests.

items.title: Sovereign wealth funds rotate further into private credit
items.description: Several large sovereign wealth funds disclosed increased allocations to private credit strategies, citing attractive risk-adjusted yields relative to public fixed income. Managers said direct-lending deal flow remains strong despite tighter underwriting standards.

items.title: Airlines and travel platforms report steady booking momentum
items.description: Major airlines and online travel platforms reported booking trends broadly in line with seasonal expectations, with premium-cabin demand outperforming economy. Fuel costs and airport capacity constraints remain the primary variables management is watching into the next quarter.

items.title: Antitrust ruling reshapes app-store fee structures
items.description: A court ruling requiring changes to mobile app-store fee and distribution policies is expected to reshape economics for developers over the coming year. Platform operators said they are reviewing the decision and evaluating compliance options.

items.title: Central banks coordinate stance on digital-asset custody rules
items.description: A group of central banks published coordinated guidance on custody standards for digital assets held by regulated institutions, aiming to reduce fragmentation across jurisdictions. Industry groups welcomed the move as a step toward clearer institutional adoption pathways.

items.title: Autonomous-driving milestone reported by major automaker
items.description: A major automaker reported a significant expansion of its autonomous-driving pilot program to additional metro areas, citing improved safety-validation data. Regulators in the relevant jurisdictions said review of the expanded rollout remains ongoing.

items.title: Government agencies expand adoption of AI data platforms
items.description: Several government agencies signed new multi-year agreements for AI-enabled data platforms, part of a broader public-sector modernization push. Vendors involved said the agreements include provisions for security review and data-residency compliance.

items.title: Currency markets react to shifting rate-cut expectations
items.description: Major currency pairs moved as traders repriced the expected pace of central-bank rate cuts following the latest economic data releases. Strategists noted positioning remains sensitive to incoming inflation and employment reports over the next several weeks.

items.title: Cybersecurity spending accelerates after high-profile breach
items.description: Enterprise cybersecurity spending accelerated following a high-profile breach disclosure, with buyers prioritizing identity and access-management upgrades. Vendors in the space reported stronger-than-expected pipeline growth heading into the next budgeting cycle.
